depression.description: I never felt lonely before, but now that I'm on my own, I struggle with this overwhelming sense of isolation. It’s hard to adjust to spending time without my ex-partner.
I feel ashamed that I’ve lost touch with friends since the divorce. It’s like they don’t understand what I’m going through, and I worry they’ll judge me.
Every time I think about my financial situation, I get anxious. The thought of running out of money and not being able to provide for Brady terrifies me.
I often feel unlovable and question why anyone would want to be around me. It’s hard to shake the feeling that I’m bound to be rejected.

Delusion.topic: Fear of being judged by others
Delusion.description: When I'm at social gatherings, I can't shake the feeling that everyone is judging me for my divorce. I feel exposed and vulnerable, like they all know my struggles.

Delusion.topic: Pressure to maintain a good image
Delusion.description: I feel this immense pressure to appear strong and put-together in front of my friends, even when I’m falling apart inside. I fear that if they see my true self, they will abandon me.
